issues.description: Hardcoded permission string 'seller_admins.read' used directly in hasPermission() call instead of using the SELLER_ADMINS_READ_PERMISSION constant that was extracted to seller-admins/domain/constants.ts in the previous cycle.
issues.fix: Import SELLER_ADMINS_READ_PERMISSION from '@/features/seller-admins/domain/constants' and use it instead of the raw string.

issues.description: Hardcoded permission string 'seller_admins.read' used in hasPermission() call in the admin app. This is a second app that should also define or import a constant for this permission string.
issues.fix: Extract a SELLER_ADMINS_READ_PERMISSION constant in apps/admin/src/features/users/domain/constants.ts and use it here. Note: the admin and studio apps are separate; cross-app import is not allowed, so each app needs its own constant.

issues.description: Local constant SELLER_ADMINS_READ = 'seller_admins.read' defined inline in the route file. While it is a local constant (not a magic string inline), it duplicates the value already defined in studio domain/constants.ts and admin domain/constants.ts. A shared domain package constant would be ideal, but since this is an API route, the local constant is acceptable per KISS.
issues.fix: Consider adding SELLER_ADMINS_READ_PERMISSION to apps/admin/src/features/users/domain/constants.ts and importing it in this route file for consistency.

patterns.description: Refactors ARCH-007 and ARCH-008 from cycle 3 were correctly applied: SELLER_ADMINS_READ_PERMISSION is properly defined in seller-admins/domain/constants.ts and used in DelegateManagementPage.tsx and ProductDelegatesPage.tsx. PRODUCT_SEARCH_DEBOUNCE_MS is correctly in products/domain/constants.ts and used in ProductFilters.tsx.
All apps/ directories follow Clean Architecture with proper feature/domain/application/infrastructure/presentation layering. No cross-feature imports detected. Route files are thin wrappers.
All apps correctly use absolute imports with @/* alias for cross-layer imports. No relative '../../' violations found in production code.
